body_txt: In a show where the main child character(s) is(are) mentioned to have divorced parents (or at least parents that aren't together anymore), it's usually the mom who has primary custody of the kid(s). However, more often than not, there will end up being an episode where the child's father visits or where the child pays a visit to their father (usually but not always as part of some custody arrangement). When the child visits his divorced father, Men Can't Keep House , Lonely Bachelor Pad or Dads Can't Cook will often feature. This rarely works in the reverse — in fiction, dads and/or father figures usually aren't depicted as raising kids on their own unless the mother of the child (or children) is dead. This might cause tension among the family in a Blended Family Drama if the parent with custody has already remarried. Compare When You Coming Home, Dad? and Disneyland Dad . Contrast Daddy Didn't Show .
